findings.title: Suspected Pre-Departure Data Exfiltration
findings.description: Rachel Thomas (customer_success) showed a 40x spike in data downloads from the customer PII datastore in the 14 days prior to simulation end. Activity concentrated in evening hours (5pm-10pm), outside normal work pattern.
detection_signals: Volume anomaly: 40x baseline download rate
Time anomaly: activity outside normal work hours
Datastore sensitivity: PII classification
Behavioral change: sustained over 14-day period
findings.mitre_technique: T1537 - Transfer Data to Cloud Account
findings.recommended_action: Suspend account, initiate DLP review, preserve logs for forensics

findings.title: Active Access by Departed Employee
findings.description: Elizabeth Miller departed on 2023-12-18T00:00:00 but their account remains active. 5 access events detected after departure, targeting sensitive datastores including PII and employee records.
detection_signals: Account active past departure date
Access 104 days post-departure
No offboarding record in identity system
MFA disabled — account potentially compromised
findings.recommended_action: Immediately disable account, audit all post-departure activity, involve HR and Legal

findings.title: Possible Credential Compromise — Foreign IP Access to Sensitive Data
findings.description: Michelle Campbell (engineering) accessed critical datastores from IP 103.21.244.161 (foreign range) between 1am-5am over 3 days. Normal access pattern is flexible from 192.168.2.97.
detection_signals: IP deviation: normal 192.168.2.97 vs suspicious 103.21.244.161
Time anomaly: 1am-5am activity, outside all work patterns
Geolocation: foreign IP range, not VPN
Data sensitivity: PHI and PCI datastores accessed
No travel notice logged in HR system
findings.recommended_action: Force password reset, revoke active sessions, enable step-up MFA, review accessed records

findings.title: Service Account Actively Using Excess Permissions
findings.description: ETL pipeline service account (svc-etl-pipeline) has permissions to 4 datastores but only requires 2 for its stated function. Over 90 days, it made 29 accesses to patient records (PHI) and billing database (PCI) — datastores with no documented ETL requirement.
detection_signals: Service account accessing PHI datastore with no documented requirement
Service account accessing PCI datastore with no documented requirement
Consistent pattern over 90 days — not one-time
No change ticket or approval found for these access patterns
Credentials not rotated in 340 days
findings.recommended_action: Scope service account to minimum required permissions, rotate credentials, audit all historical access

findings.title: PII Data Found in Unclassified Development Bucket
findings.description: Michelle Campbell copied 450MB from the customer PII datastore to the dev scratch bucket on 2024-01-20. The dev bucket has no encryption enforcement, no DLP controls, and broader access than production. 14 subsequent accesses by other engineers detected.
detection_signals: Large CopyObject from PII-classified to INTERNAL-classified bucket
Classification mismatch: PII data in non-PII bucket
Dev bucket lacks encryption at rest enforcement
Dev bucket accessible to all engineers (18 users)
Data exposed for 71 days without remediation
compliance_impact: GDPR Art.25 - Data Protection by Design
findings.recommended_action: Delete copied data immediately, add bucket policy to prevent cross-classification copies, classify dev bucket correctly

findings.title: Credentials and Secrets Accessible to Broad User Population
findings.description: The datastore 'meridian-app-configs-prod' is classified as INTERNAL but contains live credentials including Generic Password, Secret Key, Token. 6 users made 58 accesses over the simulation period. The bucket has no encryption and has not been classified since 2021-08-12 (962 days ago).
detection_signals: Datastore labeled INTERNAL contains live credentials
No encryption enforced on secrets store
6 distinct users accessed credential files
Classification not reviewed in 962 days
Contractor role has read access to this bucket
compliance_impact: CIS Control 14 - Controlled Access Based on Need to Know
findings.recommended_action: Rotate all credentials immediately. Move secrets to a dedicated secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault). Remove plaintext credentials from config files. Restrict bucket access to service accounts only with audit logging.

findings.title: Data Classification Mismatch — meridian-analytics-raw-data
findings.description: Datastore 'meridian-analytics-raw-data' is labeled 'CONFIDENTIAL' but content scanning found data types that require 'CONFIDENTIAL' classification: MRN, HICN, MBI, Birth Date. The label has not been reviewed in 669 days. 45 users currently have access based on the incorrect label — some may not be authorized for the actual data sensitivity level.
detection_signals: Content scan found MRN, HICN, MBI — exceeds CONFIDENTIAL threshold
Classification label 669 days old — exceeds 365-day review policy
45 users granted access under incorrect classification
Encryption level (AES-256) insufficient for actual data sensitivity
No re-classification triggered after content change
compliance_impact: GDPR Art.5 - Data accuracy and integrity principle
findings.recommended_action: Re-classify 'meridian-analytics-raw-data' as CONFIDENTIAL. Review and reduce access list to only users authorized for CONFIDENTIAL data. Apply appropriate encryption. Establish automated re-classification triggers on content change.

findings.title: Data Classification Mismatch — meridian-hr-documents
findings.description: Datastore 'meridian-hr-documents' is labeled 'CONFIDENTIAL' but content scanning found data types that require 'CONFIDENTIAL' classification: Social Security Number, Bank Account Number, Person, Birth Date. The label has not been reviewed in 1117 days. 17 users currently have access based on the incorrect label — some may not be authorized for the actual data sensitivity level.
detection_signals: Content scan found Social Security Number, Bank Account Number, Person — exceeds CONFIDENTIAL threshold
Classification label 1117 days old — exceeds 365-day review policy
17 users granted access under incorrect classification
Encryption level (AES-256) insufficient for actual data sensitivity
No re-classification triggered after content change
compliance_impact: GDPR Art.5 - Data accuracy and integrity principle
findings.recommended_action: Re-classify 'meridian-hr-documents' as CONFIDENTIAL. Review and reduce access list to only users authorized for CONFIDENTIAL data. Apply appropriate encryption. Establish automated re-classification triggers on content change.

findings.title: Data Classification Mismatch — meridian-app-configs-prod
findings.description: Datastore 'meridian-app-configs-prod' is labeled 'INTERNAL' but content scanning found data types that require 'INTERNAL' classification: Generic Password, Secret Key, Token, Sensitive URL. The label has not been reviewed in 962 days. 34 users currently have access based on the incorrect label — some may not be authorized for the actual data sensitivity level.
detection_signals: Content scan found Generic Password, Secret Key, Token — exceeds INTERNAL threshold
Classification label 962 days old — exceeds 365-day review policy
34 users granted access under incorrect classification
Encryption level (none) insufficient for actual data sensitivity
No re-classification triggered after content change
compliance_impact: GDPR Art.5 - Data accuracy and integrity principle
findings.recommended_action: Re-classify 'meridian-app-configs-prod' as INTERNAL. Review and reduce access list to only users authorized for INTERNAL data. Apply appropriate encryption. Establish automated re-classification triggers on content change.

findings.title: Data Classification Mismatch — meridian-data-lake-raw
findings.description: Datastore 'meridian-data-lake-raw' is labeled 'CONFIDENTIAL' but content scanning found data types that require 'CONFIDENTIAL' classification: MRN, MBI, NPI, Social Security Number. The label has not been reviewed in 1217 days. 22 users currently have access based on the incorrect label — some may not be authorized for the actual data sensitivity level.
detection_signals: Content scan found MRN, MBI, NPI — exceeds CONFIDENTIAL threshold
Classification label 1217 days old — exceeds 365-day review policy
22 users granted access under incorrect classification
Encryption level (AES-256) insufficient for actual data sensitivity
No re-classification triggered after content change
compliance_impact: GDPR Art.5 - Data accuracy and integrity principle
findings.recommended_action: Re-classify 'meridian-data-lake-raw' as CONFIDENTIAL. Review and reduce access list to only users authorized for CONFIDENTIAL data. Apply appropriate encryption. Establish automated re-classification triggers on content change.

findings.title: Sensitive Data Stored Without Encryption — meridian-dev-scratch-pad
findings.description: Datastore 'meridian-dev-scratch-pad' has sensitivity level 'Private' and contains Generic Password, Secret Key, Token but has no encryption enforced. Over the simulation period, 34 users transferred 4381.0MB of data from this store without encryption protection. Any network interception or storage access bypasses all data protection controls.
detection_signals: Encryption policy: none — data at rest and in transit unprotected
Sensitivity level Private requires encryption per policy
34 users actively reading unencrypted data
4381.0MB transferred without encryption
No remediation ticket found in change management system
compliance_impact: PCI DSS Req 3.5 - Protect stored cardholder data
SOC2 CC6.7 - Transmission and disclosure controls
findings.recommended_action: Enable encryption at rest for 'meridian-dev-scratch-pad' immediately. Enable TLS for all in-transit access. Audit all data accessed during the unencrypted period. Add encryption enforcement to bucket/database policy to prevent disabling.

findings.title: Sensitive Data Stored Without Encryption — meridian-app-configs-prod
findings.description: Datastore 'meridian-app-configs-prod' has sensitivity level 'Private' and contains Generic Password, Secret Key, Token but has no encryption enforced. Over the simulation period, 34 users transferred 9551.6MB of data from this store without encryption protection. Any network interception or storage access bypasses all data protection controls.
detection_signals: Encryption policy: none — data at rest and in transit unprotected
Sensitivity level Private requires encryption per policy
34 users actively reading unencrypted data
9551.6MB transferred without encryption
No remediation ticket found in change management system
compliance_impact: PCI DSS Req 3.5 - Protect stored cardholder data
SOC2 CC6.7 - Transmission and disclosure controls
findings.recommended_action: Enable encryption at rest for 'meridian-app-configs-prod' immediately. Enable TLS for all in-transit access. Audit all data accessed during the unencrypted period. Add encryption enforcement to bucket/database policy to prevent disabling.
